keyPoints: Structural models decompose a series into Trend, Level, and Cycle components.
Deterministic seasonal components use fixed, calendar-based patterns (e.g., dummy variables).
These models assume the seasonal effect is constant in amplitude and phase over time.
commonMistakes: Confusing deterministic seasonal patterns with stochastic ones that evolve over time.
Failing to account for a changing seasonal structure, leading to poor out-of-sample forecasts.
